evals.name: Decode Tricky PIDs
evals.description: Tests decoding of PIDs that have embedded numbers in family prefixes, CMG colors, premium finishes, and edge cases that trip up naive parsers.
evals.prompt:
Decode these GMS product IDs and tell me what each one is. For each, tell me the product family, gauge, color, finish type, and whether it's a trim, panel, or raw material:

1. CRD8109BK10
2. CO4129ARW
3. CRRC109ARW10
4. SSQ6754CMGAC
5. PCF9MCC10
6. PCF9CRBK10
7. A9AG

assertions.text: Correctly identifies CRD810 as Crown Drip 8x10 (not CRD81 + gauge 0)
Correctly identifies CO4129ARW as a 41-inch coil, 29ga, Arctic White (not a product starting with CO4)
Correctly identifies CRRC10 as Crown Ridge Cap 10in (not CRRC + gauge 1)
Correctly identifies SSQ675 as Standing Seam 675 Panel with 24ga (gauge digit 4) and CMGAC as CMG Aged Copper (standard finish, NOT crinkle)
Correctly identifies MCC as a matte finish (+8%) not crinkle (+15%)
Correctly identifies CRBK as Crinkle Black (+15%) — a CR-prefix crinkle code
Correctly identifies A9AG as Ag Panel 29ga, Ash Gray with gauge embedded in the family name (no separate gauge digit)

evals.name: Encode Products from Description
evals.description: Tests the reverse direction: generating valid PIDs from English product descriptions.
evals.prompt:
Generate GMS product IDs (PIDs) for each of these products:

1. Drip Edge, 26 gauge, Ash Gray, 12 feet
2. Ridge Cap 10 inch, 29 gauge, Arctic White, 10 feet
3. Pro Panel, 29 gauge, Black
4. Pitch Change Flashing, 26ga, Matte Black, 14 foot
5. Standing Seam 675 Panel, 24ga, CMG Charcoal

assertions.text: Drip Edge encodes to DE6AG12 (family=DE, gauge=6, color=AG, length=12)
Ridge Cap 10 inch encodes with RC10 family prefix (not just RC)
Pro Panel has NO length code (panels don't have length)
Matte Black maps to color code MBB (not BK or BKM)
24ga CMG product is correctly encoded (gauge digit 4 + CMG color code)

evals.name: Material Sourcing & Product Categories
evals.description: Tests knowledge of material source mapping (which coils feed which trims), pattern A vs B, and product categorization with margin targets.
evals.prompt:
For these GMS products, tell me: (a) what coil or flatsheet each product is made from, (b) whether it uses 20-inch or 41-inch material, (c) the product category, and (d) the target margin:

1. RC209ARW10 (Ridge Cap 20 inch, 29ga, Arctic White, 10ft)
2. HFRG6AG12 (HF Ridge Gap, 26ga, Ash Gray, 12ft)
3. PCF9BK10 (Pitch Change Flashing, 29ga, Black, 10ft)
4. SSQ5506CG (Standing Seam 550, 26ga, Charcoal Gray)
5. PRO9ARW (Pro Panel, 29ga, Arctic White)

Also explain: Why does RC20 use 41-inch material while PCF uses 20-inch material?

assertions.text: RC20 is correctly identified as Pattern B (41-inch material), sourced from CO4129ARW
HFRG uses 43-inch material (special case), sourced from CO4326AG
PCF uses Pattern A (20-inch material), sourced from CO2029BK
SSQ550 is categorized as Hidden Fastener Panel with 40% target margin
PRO is categorized as Exposed Fastener Panel with 35% target margin
Explains that RC20 needs wider material because the 20-inch ridge cap has a larger stretchout than standard trims
